behaviors.description: Scoring model applies merchant-category amount threshold and yields the expected numeric risk score when exceeded.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is deterministic rule logic (threshold lookup + score calc) inside one model component (MOCK-MISS=>Unit floor). No OS/network/subprocess needed (REAL-INFRA=no). No dependence on real AI/ML output quality (LLM-DEP=no). Higher levels rejected: behavior text is specifically 'assigns the correct risk score when ... exceeds the configured amount threshold,' which is local rule computation, not cross-service behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Call the scoring function with config containing per-category amount thresholds and a transaction above threshold; assert exact numeric score delta and threshold branch taken.

behaviors.description: Risk model detects a fraud pattern (rapid small purchases across geographies) even when explicit rule thresholds are not exceeded.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on model prediction quality for a contextual pattern, not deterministic routing (LLM-DEP=yes by behavior text 'risk model ... predict whether ... fraudulent'). A stubbed model could always pass and hide real miss-detections (MOCK-HIDE=yes). Not Workflow because this behavior is a single model inference step, not multiple agentic stages in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run repeated evaluations against the real risk model on curated fraud-pattern transactions where rule thresholds remain below limits; assert fraud probability/label crosses review cutoff at acceptable detection rate.

behaviors.description: Velocity check counts transactions per user correctly within the configured rolling time window.
behaviors.justification: Primary failure is counting/window-boundary logic (inclusive/exclusive edges, per-user grouping) inside scoring logic (MOCK-MISS=>Unit floor). No required real OS/network/subprocess (REAL-INFRA=no). No AI output dependency (LLM-DEP=no). Higher levels rejected: behavior text is 'velocity check ... counts the number of transactions within the configured time window per user,' which is algorithmic counting logic.
behaviors.test_description: Feed timestamped transactions for multiple users into velocity-check function with fixed clock/window; assert exact counts at boundary times and correct per-user isolation.

behaviors.description: If either model flags a transaction, the transaction is held for manual review (OR gate semantics).
behaviors.justification: Real failure appears at module handoff/orchestration boundary: scorer output + risk output must be combined correctly before review decision (MOCK-MISS indicates cross-module interaction). Integration brake: no real OS/network/subprocess required (a=no), no dependence on model quality since inputs can be stubbed as flagged/unflagged states (b=no), no multi-agentic chain under test (c=no). Higher levels rejected by behavior text 'operate as an OR gate, not AND'—this targets decision wiring, not external infra or model accuracy.
behaviors.test_description: Wire scoring module, risk module (stubbed outputs), and hold-decision service; set scoring=false/risk=true and assert transaction status becomes HOLD_FOR_REVIEW; include inverse cases to prevent AND regression.

behaviors.description: Combined review payload contains scoring-model numeric risk score and risk-model fraud probability with model-specific flag context.
behaviors.justification: Failure is contract mismatch when assembling outputs from multiple components into one reviewer-facing object (cross-module data wiring). Integration brake: no real OS/network/subprocess required (a=no), no need for real AI quality because fixed model outputs can validate schema/fields (b=no), no multi-step agentic pipeline required (c=no). Higher levels rejected: behavior text focuses on 'combined output includes both ... so reviewer can see which model flagged,' i.e., payload composition contract.
behaviors.test_description: Run aggregator with deterministic scorer/risk outputs and assert response includes risk_score, fraud_probability, and per-model flag/explanation fields with correct provenance.
